116	def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
117	    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False
118	
119	    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
120	    sentencepiece_model = False
121	    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
122	    created_folder = False
123	    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
124	        created_folder = True
125	        os.makedirs(file_location)
126	    pass
127	    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
128	    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
129	        sentencepiece_model = True
130	    pass
131	    if created_folder:
132	        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
133	    return sentencepiece_model

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",

1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",

1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",

2414	    self,

2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,

2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto

2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)

2502	def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
2503	    self,
2504	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2505	    tokenizer            = None,
2506	    torchao_config       = None,
2507	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2508	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2509	):
2510	    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint
2511	
2512	    Args
2513	      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
2516	    """
2517	    # first merge the lora weights
2518	    arguments = dict(locals())
2519	    arguments["model"]        = self
2520	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:
2532	        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535	
2536	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
2538	        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:
2551	        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
2552	        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
2553	        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
2554	        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
2555	    else:
2556	        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
2558	    pass
2559	    for _ in range(3):
2560	        gc.collect()
2561	pass

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the final report:

## Unsloth Codebase Structure - Comprehensive Analysis

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main package initialization
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-installation utilities
├── save.py                      # CORE SAVE/EXPORT LOGIC (105KB)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                  # Trainer extensions
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── models/                      # Model-specific implementations
├── kernels/                     # Optimized kernels (LoRA, attention, etc.)
├── registry/                    # Model registry
├── dataprep/                    # Data preparation utilities
└── utils/                       # General utilities
```

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT LOGIC FILES

**Primary File: `/unsloth/save.py` (Line 1-2679)**

This is the central hub for all save/export functionality.

#### Key Function Definitions:

| Line | Function | Purpose |
|------|----------|---------|
| 109-113 | `print_quantization_methods()` | List available GGUF quantization methods |
| 116-134 | `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` | Detect SentencePiece tokenizers |
| 159-187 | `_merge_lora()` | Core LoRA merging logic (merges A, B matrices with weight) |
| 205-760 | `unsloth_save_model()` | **Main unified save function** - handles lora/merged_16bit/merged_4bit |
| 952-1320 | `save_to_gguf()` | GGUF conversion using llama.cpp |
| 1321-1361 | `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` | Wrapper: save merged model locally |
| 1364-1407 | `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` | Wrapper: push merged model to HF Hub |
| 1768-1950 | `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | **GGUF saving method** - converts to GGUF locally |
| 1951-2128 | `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | Push GGUF to HuggingFace Hub |
| 2129-2140 | `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | Save LoRA adapters to custom directory |
| 2143-2209 | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | Convert LoRA to GGML and push |
| 2211-2274 | `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | Convert LoRA to GGML locally |
| 2276-2354 | `save_to_gguf_generic()` | Generic GGUF save for non-Llama models |
| 2356-2410 | `unsloth_generic_save()` | Generic save using merge_and_overwrite_lora |
| 2413-2453 | `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` | Generic merged save wrapper |
| 2456-2499 | `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` | Generic merged push wrapper |
| 2502-2561 | `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| 2569-2678 | `patch_saving_functions()` | **REGISTERS METHODS ON MODEL CLASS** |

---

### 3. CORE MERGING LOGIC

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:159-187` (`_merge_lora()`)

```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    # Extracts LoRA parameters from quantized or unquantized layers
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        # Dequantize if 4-bit quantized
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # Core merge: W += A.T @ (s * B.T)
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

**Key Details:**
- Uses `get_lora_parameters_bias()` from kernels to extract LoRA weights
- Handles both 4-bit quantized and full precision layers
- Uses in-place matrix multiplication (`addmm_`) for memory efficiency

---

### 4. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE REGISTERED ON MODEL CLASS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2569-2678` (`patch_saving_functions()`)

This function is called after model loading to register custom save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # Dynamically creates unsloth_push_to_hub wrapper
    # Patches model.push_to_hub method
    
    # Line 2661-2667: Attaches methods to model
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Where patch_saving_functions is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py:2188, 2204, 2902` (for language models)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py:702, 703, 918, 964` (for vision models)
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py:2309` (for tokenizers)

---

### 5. EXISTING SAVE METHODS AND THEIR IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### A. **save_pretrained_merged()** - Line 1321-1361
- **What it does:** Saves model with LoRA merged into weights (16-bit or 4-bit)
- **Parameters:**
  - `save_directory`: Local path to save
  - `tokenizer`: Optional tokenizer to save alongside
  - `save_method`: "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit", or "lora"
  - `push_to_hub`: Whether to push to HF after saving
  - `token`: HF API token
- **Internal flow:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=False`
- **Return:** None (saves to disk/hub)

#### B. **push_to_hub_merged()** - Line 1364-1407
- **What it does:** Same as save_pretrained_merged but pushes to HuggingFace Hub
- **Parameters:** Same as above but `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`
- **Internal flow:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

#### C. **save_pretrained_gguf()** - Line 1768-1950
- **What it does:** Merges LoRA, converts to float16, exports to GGUF format for llama.cpp
- **Key steps:**
  1. Installs llama.cpp if not present (lines 1835-1873)
  2. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` (line 1854)
  3. Calls `save_to_gguf()` with quantization options (line 1899)
  4. Creates Ollama Modelfile (line 1905)
- **Parameters:**
  - `quantization_method`: "fast_quantized", "quantized", "not_quantized", "q4_k_m", etc.
  - Supports multiple quantization formats
- **File output:** .gguf files in save_directory

#### D. **push_to_hub_gguf()** - Line 1951-2128
- **What it does:** Same as save_pretrained_gguf() but uploads GGUF files to HF Hub
- **Key difference:** Pushes resulting GGUF files to HuggingFace Hub

#### E. **save_pretrained_ggml()** - Line 2211-2274
- **What it does:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format (for llama.cpp LoRA inference)
- **Internal flow:**
  1. Saves LoRA adapters with `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2237)
  2. Runs `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py` (line 2242)
  3. Saves result as `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

#### F. **push_to_hub_ggml()** - Line 2143-2209
- **What it does:** Converts LoRA to GGML and pushes to HF Hub

#### G. **save_pretrained_torchao()** - Line 2502-2561
- **What it does:** Merges LoRA, then quantizes with TorchAO library
- **Flow:**
  1. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` (line 2525)
  2. Loads saved model and applies TorchAO quantization (line 2541)
  3. Saves quantized model

#### H. **push_to_hub()** (Patched) - Line 2586-2638
- **What it does:** Wraps original `push_to_hub` to add "Trained with Unsloth" tags
- **Dynamically generated** from original method signature

---

### 6. MAIN UNIFIED SAVE FUNCTION

**`unsloth_save_model()` - Line 205-760**

This is the core orchestrator handling all save scenarios:

**Parameters:**
- `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HF Hub upload
- `maximum_memory_usage`: 0.9 (90%) by default
- `temporary_location`: "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers"

**Logic flow (Line 283-760):**
```
1. Check save_method validity
2. If merged_4bit: Call model.merge_and_unload()
3. If lora OR merged_4bit with push_to_hub:
   - Use original model.push_to_hub()
   - Return early
4. Otherwise (merged_16bit or local save):
   - Merge LoRA weights layer-by-layer
   - Handle quantization state
   - Create state dict
   - Save to disk with memory management
```

**State dict creation (Line 567-610):**
- Iterates through `internal_model.model.layers`
- For each layer, processes LLAMA_WEIGHTS and LLAMA_LAYERNORMS
- Calls `_merge_lora()` for each weight projection
- Handles memory pressure by saving to disk if needed

---

### 7. GGUF CONVERSION LOGIC

**`save_to_gguf()` - Line 952-1320**

**Key processing steps:**
1. **Install llama.cpp** (non-blocking): Lines 1045-1114
2. **Validate quantization methods** (Line 1006-1023)
   - Maps shorthand ("fast_quantized" → "q8_0", "quantized" → "q4_k_m")
3. **Determine first_conversion dtype** (Line 1034-1150)
   - Ensures precision level sufficient for quantization targets
4. **Run HF→GGUF conversion** (Line 1151-1217)
   - Calls `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` or `convert_hf_to_gguf.py`
5. **Run quantization** (Line 1218-1320)
   - Calls `llama-quantize` for each requested format
   - Outputs files like `model-q4_k_m.gguf`

**Supported quantization formats:**
- `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`: Full precision formats
- `q8_0`: Fast, moderate compression
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`: Original 4-bit format
- `q4_k_s`, `q4_k_m`: Optimized 4-bit (recommended)
- `q5_k_s`, `q5_k_m`: Optimized 5-bit
- `q6_k`: 6-bit
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_*`, `q3_k_xs`: Lower bit quantizations

---

### 8. MODEL CLASS STRUCTURE

**How models expose these methods:**

**File: `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (FastLanguageModel)**
- Line 109-527: `from_pretrained()` static method
- Returns PeftModel with attached methods

**File: `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (FastLlamaModel)**
- Base model class for Llama architecture
- Line 2188, 2204, 2902: Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` after creation

**File: `/unsloth/models/vision.py`**
- Vision model support
- Lines 702, 703, 918, 964: Patches saving functions with `vision=True`

**Method attachment happens in `patch_saving_functions()`:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
# ... etc
```

---

### 9. LORA ADAPTER SAVING

**LoRA saving is handled by:**
1. **Native PEFT method** (when `save_method="lora"`)
   - Line 444-449: Calls `model.save_pretrained()` with PEFT's native save
   
2. **Custom LoRA to GGML conversion** (Line 2211-2274)
   - Uses `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` to save PEFT adapters
   - Runs llama.cpp's `convert-lora-to-ggml.py` script
   - Outputs `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

**Key files:**
- Adapter config: `adapter_config.json` (PEFT standard)
- Adapter weights: `adapter_model.bin` or `adapter_model.safetensors`

---

### 10. INITIALIZATION AND EXPORTS

**File: `/unsloth/__init__.py` (Lines 261-266)**
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *  # Exports all save functions
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

_patch_trl_trainer()  # Patches TRL for compatibility
```

**Exported from save.py (Line 48-54):**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

---

### 11. TEST FILES SHOWING USAGE

**Test: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` (Lines 124-200)**
```python
# Example: Save merged 16-bit
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Example: Save merged 4-bit
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_4bit_forced"
)
```

**Test: `/tests/qlora/test_unsloth_qlora_train_and_merge.py` (Line 192)**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(...)
```

---

### 12. SUMMARY OF SAVE METHOD CALL CHAIN

```
User calls model.save_pretrained_merged()
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() registered this method
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged() wrapper
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save() with merge_and_overwrite_lora()
    ↓
FOR LLAMA: unsloth_save_model() 
    ├─ Merges LoRA with _merge_lora()
    ├─ Creates state dict
    ├─ Saves to disk with memory management
    └─ Returns to caller

FOR GGUF: unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
    ├─ Calls unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
    ├─ Calls save_to_gguf() with quantization
    ├─ Installs llama.cpp
    ├─ Runs convert-hf-to-gguf.py
    ├─ Runs llama-quantize
    └─ Returns GGUF file paths
```

---

This comprehensive analysis reveals that Unsloth's save/export system is well-structured with:
- **Centralized logic** in `/unsloth/save.py`
- **Dynamic method registration** via `patch_saving_functions()`
- **Modular design** with generic and specialized save functions
- **Memory-efficient** merging with temporary disk buffering
- **Multiple export formats** (PEFT LoRA, Merged FP16, 4-bit merged, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO)


**Key Details:**
- Uses `get_lora_parameters_bias()` from kernels to extract LoRA weights
- Handles both 4-bit quantized and full precision layers
- Uses in-place matrix multiplication (`addmm_`) for memory efficiency

---

### 4. HOW SAVE METHODS ARE REGISTERED ON MODEL CLASS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:2569-2678` (`patch_saving_functions()`)

This function is called after model loading to register custom save methods:

```python
def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    # Dynamically creates unsloth_push_to_hub wrapper
    # Patches model.push_to_hub method
    
    # Line 2661-2667: Attaches methods to model
    if not vision:
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Where patch_saving_functions is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py:2188, 2204, 2902` (for language models)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py:702, 703, 918, 964` (for vision models)
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py:2309` (for tokenizers)

---

### 5. EXISTING SAVE METHODS AND THEIR IMPLEMENTATIONS

#### A. **save_pretrained_merged()** - Line 1321-1361
- **What it does:** Saves model with LoRA merged into weights (16-bit or 4-bit)
- **Parameters:**
  - `save_directory`: Local path to save
  - `tokenizer`: Optional tokenizer to save alongside
  - `save_method`: "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit", or "lora"
  - `push_to_hub`: Whether to push to HF after saving
  - `token`: HF API token
- **Internal flow:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=False`
- **Return:** None (saves to disk/hub)

#### B. **push_to_hub_merged()** - Line 1364-1407
- **What it does:** Same as save_pretrained_merged but pushes to HuggingFace Hub
- **Parameters:** Same as above but `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`
- **Internal flow:** Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

#### C. **save_pretrained_gguf()** - Line 1768-1950
- **What it does:** Merges LoRA, converts to float16, exports to GGUF format for llama.cpp
- **Key steps:**
  1. Installs llama.cpp if not present (lines 1835-1873)
  2. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` (line 1854)
  3. Calls `save_to_gguf()` with quantization options (line 1899)
  4. Creates Ollama Modelfile (line 1905)
- **Parameters:**
  - `quantization_method`: "fast_quantized", "quantized", "not_quantized", "q4_k_m", etc.
  - Supports multiple quantization formats
- **File output:** .gguf files in save_directory

#### D. **push_to_hub_gguf()** - Line 1951-2128
- **What it does:** Same as save_pretrained_gguf() but uploads GGUF files to HF Hub
- **Key difference:** Pushes resulting GGUF files to HuggingFace Hub

#### E. **save_pretrained_ggml()** - Line 2211-2274
- **What it does:** Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format (for llama.cpp LoRA inference)
- **Internal flow:**
  1. Saves LoRA adapters with `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (line 2237)
  2. Runs `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py` (line 2242)
  3. Saves result as `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

#### F. **push_to_hub_ggml()** - Line 2143-2209
- **What it does:** Converts LoRA to GGML and pushes to HF Hub

#### G. **save_pretrained_torchao()** - Line 2502-2561
- **What it does:** Merges LoRA, then quantizes with TorchAO library
- **Flow:**
  1. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` (line 2525)
  2. Loads saved model and applies TorchAO quantization (line 2541)
  3. Saves quantized model

#### H. **push_to_hub()** (Patched) - Line 2586-2638
- **What it does:** Wraps original `push_to_hub` to add "Trained with Unsloth" tags
- **Dynamically generated** from original method signature

---

### 6. MAIN UNIFIED SAVE FUNCTION

**`unsloth_save_model()` - Line 205-760**

This is the core orchestrator handling all save scenarios:

**Parameters:**
- `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
- `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HF Hub upload
- `maximum_memory_usage`: 0.9 (90%) by default
- `temporary_location`: "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers"

**Logic flow (Line 283-760):**
```
1. Check save_method validity
2. If merged_4bit: Call model.merge_and_unload()
3. If lora OR merged_4bit with push_to_hub:
   - Use original model.push_to_hub()
   - Return early
4. Otherwise (merged_16bit or local save):
   - Merge LoRA weights layer-by-layer
   - Handle quantization state
   - Create state dict
   - Save to disk with memory management
```

**State dict creation (Line 567-610):**
- Iterates through `internal_model.model.layers`
- For each layer, processes LLAMA_WEIGHTS and LLAMA_LAYERNORMS
- Calls `_merge_lora()` for each weight projection
- Handles memory pressure by saving to disk if needed

---

### 7. GGUF CONVERSION LOGIC

**`save_to_gguf()` - Line 952-1320**

**Key processing steps:**
1. **Install llama.cpp** (non-blocking): Lines 1045-1114
2. **Validate quantization methods** (Line 1006-1023)
   - Maps shorthand ("fast_quantized" → "q8_0", "quantized" → "q4_k_m")
3. **Determine first_conversion dtype** (Line 1034-1150)
   - Ensures precision level sufficient for quantization targets
4. **Run HF→GGUF conversion** (Line 1151-1217)
   - Calls `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` or `convert_hf_to_gguf.py`
5. **Run quantization** (Line 1218-1320)
   - Calls `llama-quantize` for each requested format
   - Outputs files like `model-q4_k_m.gguf`

**Supported quantization formats:**
- `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`: Full precision formats
- `q8_0`: Fast, moderate compression
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`: Original 4-bit format
- `q4_k_s`, `q4_k_m`: Optimized 4-bit (recommended)
- `q5_k_s`, `q5_k_m`: Optimized 5-bit
- `q6_k`: 6-bit
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_*`, `q3_k_xs`: Lower bit quantizations

---

### 8. MODEL CLASS STRUCTURE

**How models expose these methods:**

**File: `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (FastLanguageModel)**
- Line 109-527: `from_pretrained()` static method
- Returns PeftModel with attached methods

**File: `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (FastLlamaModel)**
- Base model class for Llama architecture
- Line 2188, 2204, 2902: Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` after creation

**File: `/unsloth/models/vision.py`**
- Vision model support
- Lines 702, 703, 918, 964: Patches saving functions with `vision=True`

**Method attachment happens in `patch_saving_functions()`:**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
# ... etc
```

---

### 9. LORA ADAPTER SAVING

**LoRA saving is handled by:**
1. **Native PEFT method** (when `save_method="lora"`)
   - Line 444-449: Calls `model.save_pretrained()` with PEFT's native save
   
2. **Custom LoRA to GGML conversion** (Line 2211-2274)
   - Uses `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` to save PEFT adapters
   - Runs llama.cpp's `convert-lora-to-ggml.py` script
   - Outputs `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

**Key files:**
- Adapter config: `adapter_config.json` (PEFT standard)
- Adapter weights: `adapter_model.bin` or `adapter_model.safetensors`

---

### 10. INITIALIZATION AND EXPORTS

**File: `/unsloth/__init__.py` (Lines 261-266)**
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *  # Exports all save functions
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

_patch_trl_trainer()  # Patches TRL for compatibility
```

**Exported from save.py (Line 48-54):**
```python
__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

---

### 11. TEST FILES SHOWING USAGE

**Test: `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` (Lines 124-200)**
```python
# Example: Save merged 16-bit
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Example: Save merged 4-bit
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_4bit_forced"
)
```

**Test: `/tests/qlora/test_unsloth_qlora_train_and_merge.py` (Line 192)**
```python
model.save_pretrained_merged(...)
```

---

### 12. SUMMARY OF SAVE METHOD CALL CHAIN

```
User calls model.save_pretrained_merged()
    ↓
patch_saving_functions() registered this method
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged() wrapper
    ↓
unsloth_generic_save() with merge_and_overwrite_lora()
    ↓
FOR LLAMA: unsloth_save_model() 
    ├─ Merges LoRA with _merge_lora()
    ├─ Creates state dict
    ├─ Saves to disk with memory management
    └─ Returns to caller

FOR GGUF: unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()
    ├─ Calls unsloth_save_model(..., save_method="merged_16bit")
    ├─ Calls save_to_gguf() with quantization
    ├─ Installs llama.cpp
    ├─ Runs convert-hf-to-gguf.py
    ├─ Runs llama-quantize
    └─ Returns GGUF file paths
```

---

This comprehensive analysis reveals that Unsloth's save/export system is well-structured with:
- **Centralized logic** in `/unsloth/save.py`
- **Dynamic method registration** via `patch_saving_functions()`
- **Modular design** with generic and specialized save functions
- **Memory-efficient** merging with temporary disk buffering
- **Multiple export formats** (PEFT LoRA, Merged FP16, 4-bit merged, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO)

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return

del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
pass

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass

def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,

    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,

    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\

elif model_type == "qwen3moe":  apply_lora_mlp = apply_lora_mlp_swiglu
        else:
            raise NotImplementedError(f"Unsloth: {model_type} is not yet implemented!")
        pass

        model = prepare_model_for_kbit_training(
            model,
            use_gradient_checkpointing = use_gradient_checkpointing,
            use_reentrant = True,
        )

        # Fix up config for transformers uploading PEFT
        for active_adapter in model.peft_config.keys():
            # Not necessary since we requires transformers >= 4.37
            if False:
                name = model.peft_config[active_adapter].base_model_name_or_path
                if name.startswith("unsloth/") and name.endswith("-bnb-4bit"):
                    name = name[:len(name) - len("-bnb-4bit")]
                    model.peft_config[active_adapter].base_model_name_or_path = name
                pass
            # Add revision to enable future fast inference paths
            # [TODO] Bugs out!see https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/492
            # model.peft_config[active_adapter].revision = f"unsloth"
        pass

        from transformers.trainer import Trainer
        if Trainer._inner_training_loop.__name__ != "_fast_inner_training_loop":
            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Unsuccessfully patched Trainer! Please file a bug report!")
        pass

from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
